authors: Masi, C. M.; Chen, H. Y.; Hawkley, L. C.; Cacioppo, J. T.
title: A Meta-Analysis of Interventions to Reduce Loneliness
intervention_category: mixed (4 categories compared: social skills, social support, social interaction, maladaptive cognition)
intervention_description: Systematic review and meta-analysis of 50 studies (20 RCTs, 18 non-RCTs, 12 pre-post) testing loneliness interventions classified into four categories.
key_finding: Interventions targeting maladaptive social cognition produced the largest effect sizes (d = -0.60 for RCTs) compared to social skills training, enhanced social support, or increased social interaction.
notes: Foundational meta-analysis; introduced the influential 4-category taxonomy of loneliness interventions still widely used today.

authors: Käll, A.; Jägholm, S.; Hesser, H.; Andersson, F.; Mathaldi, A.; Norkvist, B. T.; Shafran, R.; Andersson, G.
title: Internet-Based Cognitive Behavior Therapy for Loneliness: A Pilot Randomized Controlled Trial
intervention_description: 8-week therapist-guided internet-delivered CBT program targeting loneliness via psychoeducation, behavioral experiments, and cognitive restructuring of maladaptive interpersonal cognitions.
key_finding: Guided internet-delivered CBT produced a large reduction in loneliness vs. waitlist (d = 0.77); gains maintained at 4-month follow-up.
risk_of_bias: some concerns (pilot; small sample; unblinded participants)
notes: Pilot RCT; informed larger subsequent trials of iCBT for loneliness. Effect size reported favors the intervention (positive d = reduction in loneliness).
